mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with t-stat > 2.0, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved operational efficiency.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 during high-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and persistent consumer price inflation above 3.5% year-over-year creates stagflationary pressure, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 3.5%, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend confirms macroeconomic expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters, or rolling Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 4-quarter windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surges > 200k fail to generate positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances, or the signal reverses direction when UNRATE > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate synchronized demand and supply contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets anticipate earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and RSAFS produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.
